title: fix port -l timing with healthchecks
body:
many of the port tests use our nginx container image.  in some cases, we have timing
issues between when the nginx and the container are running and when the port -l
command is run causing test flakes.  we now use the container image's built in
healthcheck to ensure that nginx is running (and subsequently the container
itself) before running the port command.

Fixes: #3309

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

@baude have you figured out why #3313 passed CI even though `podman healthcheck run` could not have worked on that image? Without understanding that, I'm a little worried that there might be something going on that isn't actually running CI tests, or is masking failures. Any idea how those earlier tests passed?

> @baude have you figured out why #3313 passed CI even though `podman healthcheck run` could not have worked on that image? Without understanding that, I'm a little worried that there might be something going on that isn't actually running CI tests, or is masking failures. Any idea how those earlier tests passed?

I'm not going to spend any time worrying about why a test passed when it shouldn't have ... I'm more focussed on fixing the flake that we do know about.  @mheon has been bugging me to use this very approach for some time now.
LGTM, tests are reporting timing out trying to reach github

>why #3313 passed CI even though podman healthcheck run could not have worked on that image?

Problem solved: TL;DR because `podman load` restores the healthcheck.

Longer version: CI tests only pull images once, then cache them all in `/tmp` using `podman save`. Then, as needed, images are restored using `podman load`. This actually restores `Healthcheck`, possibly because of some OCI-vs-Docker-format thing I'm not clueful about.
```
# podman pull -q quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest
3ef70f7291f47dfe2b82931a993e16f5a44a0e7a68034c3e0e086d77f5829adc

# podman create quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest
fb78661f9a4f69f2a2d41da7f2f58bb575a7bfa67e20ee7b6cfb3c2025539151

# podman inspect -l |grep -A3 Healthcheck
            "Healthcheck": {
                "Status": "",
                "FailingStreak": 0,
                "Log": null
```
Now save and load:
```
# podman save -o /tmp/alpine_nginx-latest.tar quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest

# podman rmi quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest
3ef70f7291f47dfe2b82931a993e16f5a44a0e7a68034c3e0e086d77f5829adc

# podman load -q -i /tmp/alpine_nginx-latest.tar
Loaded image(s): quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest

# podman create quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest
7528b88871281287e2664c2327fcd70be0a40f283145b590800f20867c2ad5cc

# podman inspect -l |grep -A3 Healthcheck
            "Healthcheck": {
                "Status": "",
                "FailingStreak": 0,
                "Log": null
--
            "Healthcheck": {
                "Test": [
                    "CMD-SHELL",
                    "curl -f http://localhost/ || exit 1"
```
This explains, to my satisfaction, why CI passed on #3313. I had felt some concern that there might be a hidden problem in the ginkgo tests causing test failures to be silently suppressed. (There is -- see #3366 -- but that's a separate issue). 

Thanks for listening.
